support.title: Support period
support.description: CRA Annex I Part II requires you to provide security updates throughout the product's support period — minimum 5 years.
support.updateChannelPlaceholder: e.g. OTA, package manager, download portal

start.title: Support period start
start.body: The date from which you commit to providing security updates. Usually the same as the product's launch date or first placing on the market.
start.ref: CRA Annex I Part II(8) — manufacturers must provide security updates "during a period of time proportionate to the expected use of the product," and in any case at least 5 years from placing on the market (Article 13).

end.title: Support period end
end.body: The last date you will ship security updates for this product. Must be at least 5 years after the support start date; longer is acceptable (and often expected for industrial/medical devices).
end.ref: CRA Article 13 — the support period is a binding commitment declared to end users and printed on the end-user cybersecurity information sheet. Ending support earlier than declared is a breach of the CRA.

updateChannel.title: Update channel
updateChannel.body: How security updates reach end users — Over-The-Air (OTA), package manager (apt/npm), portal download, manual replacement, etc. End-users need to know this to stay protected.
updateChannel.ref: CRA Annex II(3) of the end-user information — the declared support period is meaningless unless users also know how updates are delivered. Required on the end-user cybersecurity information sheet.

list.title: Release history
list.confirmDelete: Delete this release? This cannot be undone.

empty.title: No releases logged
empty.description: Publish a release to record security updates, fixed CVEs, and signed digests in your CRA audit trail.

new.title: Publish a new release
new.description: Log the version, date, and CVEs fixed so your audit trail shows the support commitment is being met.

version.title: Version
version.body: Semver or internal version identifier (e.g. 1.2.3, 2024-04-17-build42). Must uniquely identify this specific build — users and auditors use it to tell whether a given device is patched.
version.ref: CRA Annex II(2) — the technical documentation must include version identifiers traceable from the product to this record.

releasedAt.title: Release date
releasedAt.body: The date you made this version generally available to end users — not when it was merged to main or when the build completed. Used to measure CRA's "without undue delay" requirement for security updates.
releasedAt.ref: CRA Article 13(8) — security updates must be made available "without undue delay and free of charge." This date is the auditable timestamp.

type.title: Release type
type.body: Security = at least one patched vulnerability. Bug fix = non-security bugs. Feature = new functionality. Maintenance = internal cleanup, no user-visible changes. "Security" auto-flags the release as a security update in the CRA evidence ledger.
type.ref: CRA Article 13(8) — security updates have distinct obligations (free, without undue delay, communicated to users). Tagging correctly here is what makes those obligations measurable.

cves.title: CVEs fixed
cves.body: List every CVE this release patches — comma or space separated. Links to the vulnerability ledger and closes the loop from detection → triage → fix. Blank is fine for non-security releases.
cves.ref: CRA Article 13(8) — security updates must address known exploitable vulnerabilities. Keeping this field accurate is the evidence an auditor will ask for.

notes.title: Release notes
notes.body: Customer-facing summary. Plain language, include upgrade instructions and any breaking changes. Published on the download portal and attached to the end-user cybersecurity information sheet.
notes.ref: CRA Annex II(3) of the end-user information — users need enough information to decide whether to apply the update.

digest.title: Signed digest
digest.body: SHA-256 hash of the signed artifact or the identity of the signer (e.g. GPG key fingerprint, Sigstore identity). Proof of integrity that lets a customer verify the binary they downloaded is the one you shipped.
digest.ref: CRA Annex I Part I(3)(h) — products must include "integrity protection of stored, transmitted, and processed data." A signed release digest is how this is commonly evidenced for software.
